title: kanbus_clock frame rate drops when gossip workers hot-restart
description:
Observed: clock appears to update at a lower frame rate after enabling multi-root gossip mode. Logs show repeated non-stop restart loops for failing gossip watch workers (rc=1), which can consume CPU and starve rendering.

Expected: failing gossip roots should back off retries instead of hot-looping, so clock rendering cadence remains stable.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Investigating restart storm in _watch_gossip_worker and implementing exponential restart backoff to protect render cadence.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Implemented fix: gossip worker restart backoff now exponential and bounded (0.5s -> 1 -> 2 -> 4 -> ... up to 30s) instead of hot-looping every 0.5s. Added test coverage for delay function, ran py_compile + pytest tests/test_kanbus_clock.py (54 passed), and restarted clock process for live verification.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Additional isolation landed: moved blocking work off the render event loop. Gossip path now runs summarize_event/kbs show via asyncio.to_thread, and auto-theme polling runs system detection via asyncio.to_thread as well. Validation: py_compile + tests/test_kanbus_clock.py (54 passed).

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Further stabilization: added theme-change hysteresis (requires two consecutive detections before applying) to prevent light/dark flapping rerenders, and auto-disables chronically failing gossip roots after repeated failures. Revalidated: py_compile + pytest tests/test_kanbus_clock.py (54 passed).

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Smoothness check event after watcher isolation

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Root cause found for ~1 FPS feel: ReactClockScene cache key was second-quantized. Fixed by keying on sub-second frame buckets tied to demo FPS and passing fractional seconds into runtime clock props. Also tuned refresh loop cadence to target FPS. Validation: py_compile + tests/test_kanbus_clock.py (55 passed).

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Implementing render-loop fix: remove per-frame page.goto in React clock runtime; use persistent page + in-page prop updates to restore >1 FPS.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Patched runtime frame path: React runtime now exposes __pixooApplyClockArgs and Python renderer keeps one Playwright page loaded, updating props in-page instead of per-frame navigation. Local benchmark: 30 frames in 1.00s (~30 FPS render path). Validation: py_compile + tests/test_kanbus_clock.py (55 passed). Waiting for device confirmation of improved clock FPS.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Verified fix: React runtime now updates in-page without per-frame navigation, restoring smooth clock refresh. User confirmed FPS is better; closing.
